title: वृन्दावन स्तुति
devanagariText:
॥ श्री वृन्दावन स्तुति ॥

वृन्दावनं सकलधाम महत्तरं यत्
कृष्णस्य वासगृहमद्भुतदिव्यलीलम्।
राधाविहारनिलयं सुखदं सुरम्यं
वन्दे सदा व्रजधरां परमां प्रसन्नाम्॥ १॥

यत्र कृष्ण रासलीलां
करोति गोपिकाः सह।
यमुनापुलिनोपान्ते
वृन्दावनं नमाम्यहम्॥ २॥

तुलसीवनसम्पन्नं कदम्बतरुशोभितम्।
गोवर्धनगिरेः पार्श्वे वृन्दावनं नमाम्यहम्॥ ३॥

नन्दगोपव्रजवासं गोपगोपीसमाकुलम्।
गवां चारणभूमिं तां वृन्दावनं नमाम्यहम्॥ ४॥

वंशीवटतलं रम्यं सेवाकुञ्जसमन्वितम्।
कालिन्दीतटसंशोभं वृन्दावनं नमाम्यहम्॥ ५॥

यत्र पादरजः कृष्णस्य सर्वत्र विराजते।
पवित्रतमभूभागं वृन्दावनं नमाम्यहम्॥ ६॥

भक्तिरसामृतसिन्धोरुत्पत्तिस्थानमुत्तमम्।
प्रेमधामनिवासं तं वृन्दावनं नमाम्यहम्॥ ७॥

वृन्दावनस्तुतिमिमां यः पठेद्भक्तिभावतः।
कृष्णप्रेमरसं प्राप्य वृन्दावनधामं व्रजेत्॥ ८॥

॥ इति श्री वृन्दावन स्तुति सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Vrindavan Stuti ||

Vrindavanam sakala-dhama mahattaram yat
Krishnasya vasa-griham-adbhuta-divya-lilam |
Radha-vihara-nilayam sukhadam suramyam
Vande sada Vraja-dharam paramam prasannam || 1 ||

Yatra Krishna rasa-lilam
Karoti gopikah saha |
Yamuna-pulinoopante
Vrindavanam namamyaham || 2 ||

Tulasi-vana-sampannam kadamba-taru-shobhitam |
Govardhana-gireh parshve Vrindavanam namamyaham || 3 ||

Nanda-gopa-Vraja-vasam gopa-gopi-samakulam |
Gavam charana-bhumim tam Vrindavanam namamyaham || 4 ||

Vamshi-vata-talam ramyam seva-kunja-samanvitam |
Kalindi-tata-samshobham Vrindavanam namamyaham || 5 ||

Yatra pada-rajah Krishnasya sarvatra virajate |
Pavitratama-bhu-bhagam Vrindavanam namamyaham || 6 ||

Bhakti-rasamrita-sindhor-utpatti-sthanam-uttamam |
Prema-dhama-nivasam tam Vrindavanam namamyaham || 7 ||

Vrindavan-stutim-imam yah pathed-bhakti-bhavatah |
Krishna-prema-rasam prapya Vrindavan-dhamam vrajet || 8 ||

|| Iti Shri Vrindavan Stuti Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
वृन्दावन, सभी धामों में सबसे महान, कृष्ण का निवास गृह, अद्भुत दिव्य लीलास्थल, राधा के विहार का निलय, सुखद और सुरम्य - उस परम प्रसन्न व्रजभूमि को सदा वन्दना करता हूँ॥ १॥

जहाँ कृष्ण गोपिकाओं के साथ रासलीला करते हैं, यमुना के पुलिन (बालू तट) के समीप, उस वृन्दावन को प्रणाम करता हूँ॥ २॥

तुलसी वन से सम्पन्न, कदम्ब वृक्षों से शोभित, गोवर्धन गिरि के समीप स्थित वृन्दावन को प्रणाम करता हूँ॥ ३॥

नन्द गोप के व्रज निवास, गोप-गोपियों से भरे हुए, गायों की चारण भूमि, उस वृन्दावन को प्रणाम करता हूँ॥ ४॥

रम्य वंशीवट तल, सेवाकुंज से सुसज्जित, कालिन्दी (यमुना) के तट से शोभित वृन्दावन को प्रणाम करता हूँ॥ ५॥

जहाँ कृष्ण की चरण रज सर्वत्र विराजती है, उस पवित्रतम भूभाग वृन्दावन को प्रणाम करता हूँ॥ ६॥

भक्तिरसामृतसिन्धु की उत्पत्ति का उत्तम स्थान, प्रेमधाम का निवास, उस वृन्दावन को प्रणाम करता हूँ॥ ७॥

जो भक्ति भाव से इस वृन्दावन स्तुति का पाठ करता है, वह कृष्ण प्रेम रस प्राप्त कर वृन्दावन धाम को प्राप्त करता है॥ ८॥
benefits: Prayer to the sacred land of Vrindavan Dham
Invokes the spiritual energy of Krishna's abode
Invokes the blessing of krishna-prema through devotion to the dham
Ideal for recitation during Vrindavan pilgrimage
Deepens connection with Radha-Krishna's divine pastimes
Traditionally associated with spiritual joy and inner peace
description: Vrindavan Stuti — वृन्दावन स्तुति — is a devotional hymn of praise offered to Lord Krishna. Rooted in Gaudiya Vaishnava Tradition, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Regular recitation is believed to bring spiritual joy and inner peace, invoke the spiritual energy of Krishna's abode, and bestow Krishna-prema through devotion to the Dham. For best results, devotees chant this on Wednesday (Budhvar) and Friday (Shukravar) and during Janmashtami, Holi, and Kartik Purnima. At 8 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 7 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Vrindavan Stuti in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Vrindavan Stuti - prayer to the sacred Vrindavan Dham in Sanskrit with transliteration and Hindi meaning. Invokes blessings of Radha-Krishna.

Krishna Radha rasa lila Vrindavan story

Seva Kunja Nidhuvan Krishna lila places

seoFaqs.question: What is the Vrindavan Stuti and to which tradition does it belong?
seoFaqs.answer: The Vrindavan Stuti is a Sanskrit hymn of praise dedicated to Vrindavana — the sacred forest-dham on the banks of the Yamuna in Mathura district, Uttar Pradesh, where Lord Krishna spent his childhood and youth performing the divine lilas with the gopis, especially Srimati Radharani. The hymn opens Vrindavanam sakala-dham mahattaram yat Krishnasya vasa-griham adbhuta-divya-lilam — Vrindavana, the supreme of all dhamas, the residence of Krishna, the place of wonderful divine lilas. It belongs to the Vrindavana-mahatmya stuti corpus preserved in the Garga Samhita, the Brahma Vaivarta Purana and the Sri Vrindavana Mahimamrita of Prabodhananda Saraswati. (Sources: Garga Samhita Vrindavan Khanda; Brahma Vaivarta Purana Krishna Janma Khanda; Sri Vrindavan Mahimamrita.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is Vrindavan considered the supreme dham in Vaishnava tradition?
seoFaqs.answer: Vaishnava tradition, especially the Gaudiya school of Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, holds Vrindavana as the highest of all dhamas — even higher than Vaikuntha (the eternal abode of Vishnu) — because it is the place where Krishna's parakiya-rasa madhurya-lila with Radha and the gopis is eternally enacted. The earthly Vrindavana of present-day Mathura district is held to be the manifestation in this world of Goloka Vrindavana, the spiritual planet at the apex of all spiritual worlds in Bhagavata cosmology. The verse Vrindavanam sakala-dham mahattaram literally declares Vrindavana the greatest of all dhamas. The forest itself, the Yamuna, the kunds, the trees, the cows and even the dust of Vrindavana are considered worthy of supreme worship. (Sources: Garga Samhita; Brahma Samhita 5; Sri Chaitanya Charitamrita.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the principal lila-sthalas (sacred sites) within Vrindavana?
seoFaqs.answer: Vrindavana contains numerous lila-sthalas associated with specific Krishna pastimes. The principal sites include Banke Bihari Mandir (the most popular contemporary darshan), Sri Radha Raman Mandir (Gopal Bhatta Goswami's deity), Sri Radha Damodar Mandir (Jiva Goswami's seat), Sri Radha Govinda Mandir (Rupa Goswami's deity, original now in Jaipur), Sri Madana Mohan Mandir (Sanatana Goswami's deity), Nidhuvan (the eternal nightly rasa-sthali), Seva Kunja, Imli Tala, Kaliya Daha (where Krishna subdued the serpent Kaliya), Cheer Ghat (where Krishna stole the gopis' clothes), Kesi Ghat, Govardhan (twenty-six kilometres away), Radha Kund and Shyama Kund. The traditional Vrindavana parikrama covers approximately ten kilometres around the town. (Sources: Vraja Mandala parikrama guides; ISKCON Vrindavana darshan.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the Vraja Mandala parikrama and how does Vrindavan fit in it?
seoFaqs.answer: The Vraja Mandala parikrama is the traditional eighty-four-kos (approximately 252 kilometres) circumambulation of the entire Vraja Bhumi associated with Krishna's lila — covering Mathura (his birthplace), Gokul (his infancy), Vrindavana (his youth), Govardhan (the lifted hill), Nandagaon (Nanda Maharaj's village), Barsana (Radharani's birthplace), Varsana, Kaman, Sanket and dozens of smaller villages and tirthas. The parikrama, traditionally completed in forty days during Karthika or Phalguna months, is led by Vaishnava parties singing kirtan and reciting the Vrindavan Stuti at each lila-sthala. Vrindavana itself is the central jewel of this circuit. (Sources: Garga Samhita; Vraja Mandala Bhraman traditional guides; ISKCON parikrama.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how should Vrindavan Stuti be chanted?
seoFaqs.answer: The Vrindavan Stuti is chanted daily by Vaishnava devotees as part of their morning sadhana, especially during the brahma muhurta. It acquires special potency during the four-month Chaturmasya vow (Ashadha to Karthika), the entire Karthika maas (October to November) considered the most sacred month for Vrindavana parikrama, on Janmashtami (Krishna's birth on Bhadrapada Krishna Ashtami), Radha Ashtami (Bhadrapada Shukla Ashtami, Radharani's birth), Govardhan Puja (Karthika Shukla Pratipada), and the Vrindavan Bihari Pancharatra festivals. Devotees on the Vraja Mandala parikrama recite it at every kunja and kund as they walk. Pilgrims at Banke Bihari Mandir chant it during the four daily darshans. (Sources: Drikpanchang Vaishnava calendar; ISKCON Vrindavan schedule.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is the principal deity of contemporary Vrindavan worship?
seoFaqs.answer: While Vrindavana hosts hundreds of Krishna deities, the most popular contemporary darshan is Sri Banke Bihari, the tribhanga (three-bend) form of Krishna installed in 1864 in the Banke Bihari Mandir by Swami Haridas. The deity is so charged with bhakti-shakti that the curtain in front of the deity is repeatedly drawn and opened every few seconds during darshan to prevent devotees from being overcome and to prevent the deity himself, said to be alive and tempted to follow particularly devoted darshanis home, from leaving the temple. Other principal deities include Sri Radha Raman, Sri Radha Damodar, Sri Madana Mohan, Sri Govinda Deva, ISKCON's Sri Sri Krishna Balaram and Sri Sri Radha Shyamasundara. (Sources: Banke Bihari Mandir history; Vrindavana temple guides.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does Vrindavan Stuti recitation grant?
seoFaqs.answer: Tradition declares that recitation of Vrindavan Stuti is itself a form of nama-rupa-lila-dham smarana — remembering the name, form, lila and abode of Krishna — which is the four-fold meditation prescribed by the Gaudiya acharyas as the supreme means to bhakti. Specifically, the recitation grants vrindavana-vasa-phala (the merit of physically residing in Vrindavana), the awakening of madhurya-bhava (the romantic devotional sentiment), liberation from samsara, the grace of Radha-Krishna, and ultimately entry into the eternal Goloka Vrindavana after death. Even the recitation by those who cannot physically visit is said to grant the same merits as the parikrama. (Sources: Sri Vrindavan Mahimamrita; Brahma Samhita 5.56; Bhagavata Purana 10.)
